user_query: On fathers third day, add a superfood breakfast
raw_response:
{
  "operation": "CREATE",

  "selected_nodes": [

  ],
  "result": "<?xml version='1.0' encoding='utf-8'?>
<patch_info />
<conversation_history />
<Person name="Father">
  <Day index="1">
    <Breakfast>
      <Meal>
        <name>Greek Protein Power Bowl</name>
        <short_description>A high-protein Greek-style breakfast bowl loaded with thick strained yogurt, hard-boiled eggs, feta, chickpeas, and olive oil — over 40g of protein to fuel the morning.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Spoon a generous portion of thick Greek yogurt into a bowl. Halve two hard-boiled eggs and arrange on top with crumbled feta, a handful of roasted chickpeas, sliced cucumber, and cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with extra-virgin olive oil and sprinkle with dried oregano and a pinch of za'atar. Serve with a wedge of whole-wheat pita on the side.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="Greek yogurt" />
          <item name="hard-boiled eggs" />
          <item name="feta cheese" />
          <item name="roasted chickpeas" />
          <item name="cucumber" />
          <item name="cherry tomatoes" />
          <item name="extra-virgin olive oil" />
          <item name="oregano" />
          <item name="whole-wheat pita" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Exceptionally high in protein: Greek yogurt (~17g per cup), two eggs (~12g), feta (~6g), and chickpeas (~7g) combine for 40g+ protein. Chickpeas add fiber and iron; olive oil provides heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. Cucumber and tomatoes supply hydration and vitamins C and K. A true Greek-inspired powerhouse breakfast for sustained energy and muscle support.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Shakshuka — Mediterranean Sunrise</name>
        <short_description>Eggs poached in a spiced tomato-pepper sauce with cumin and paprika, served with crusty bread for dipping — a vibrant, savory Mediterranean breakfast.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Sauté diced onion, garlic, and bell pepper in olive oil. Add crushed tomatoes, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of chili flakes; simmer 10 minutes. Create wells and crack eggs into the sauce, cover and cook until whites are set. Sprinkle with crumbled feta and fresh parsley. Serve with warm crusty bread or pita for dipping.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="eggs" />
          <item name="crushed tomatoes" />
          <item name="bell pepper" />
          <item name="onion" />
          <item name="garlic" />
          <item name="cumin" />
          <item name="paprika" />
          <item name="feta cheese" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
          <item name="crusty bread" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Eggs provide high-quality protein and choline; tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; bell peppers add additional vitamin C and antioxidants. Cumin and paprika offer anti-inflammatory compounds. Olive oil supplies healthy fats. A moderate-calorie, nutrient-dense breakfast; control sodium through feta portions.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Mediterranean Fig & Honey Oatmeal</name>
        <short_description>Warm oatmeal topped with dried figs, walnuts, honey, and a swirl of tahini — a Mediterranean twist on a classic breakfast with wholesome flavors.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Cook oats in milk or water until creamy. Top with sliced dried figs, crushed walnuts, a drizzle of raw honey, and a spoonful of tahini. Finish with a light sprinkle of cinnamon and a few pomegranate seeds if available. Pair with a small glass of fresh orange juice for a complete Mediterranean morning.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="oats" />
          <item name="dried figs" />
          <item name="walnuts" />
          <item name="honey" />
          <item name="tahini" />
          <item name="cinnamon" />
          <item name="milk" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Oats provide soluble fiber (beta-glucan) for heart health and steady blood sugar; walnuts are rich in omega-3 ALA and vitamin E; figs supply potassium, calcium, and iron; tahini adds healthy fats, protein, and calcium. Honey adds natural sweetness — use sparingly. A well-rounded Mediterranean breakfast with complex carbs, healthy fats, and fiber.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Breakfast>
    <Lunch>
      <Meal>
        <name>Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate</name>
        <short_description>Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plate.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic for at least 30 minutes. Thread onto skewers and grill until charred. Serve alongside fluffy couscous, a salad of diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and kalamata olives, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Add a lemon wedge and warm pita on the side.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="chicken breast" />
          <item name="couscous" />
          <item name="cucumber" />
          <item name="tomato" />
          <item name="red onion" />
          <item name="kalamata olives" />
          <item name="tzatziki" />
          <item name="lemon" />
          <item name="oregano" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean chicken breast is high in protein and low in saturated fat; couscous provides quick-digesting carbohydrates; cucumber and tomato add hydration, vitamins C and K; olives supply monounsaturated fats; tzatziki (yogurt-based) contributes calcium and probiotics. A balanced Mediterranean plate with protein, carbs, healthy fats, and vegetables.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad</name>
        <short_description>A hearty salad of green lentils tossed with sun-dried tomatoes, feta, arugula, and a bright lemon-herb vinaigrette — plant-powered and filling.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Cook green lentils until tender but not mushy; drain and cool slightly. Toss with chopped sun-dried tomatoes, crumbled feta, baby arugula, diced cucumber, and fresh dill. Dress with a vinaigrette of olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and minced garlic. Serve at room temperature; keeps well for meal prep.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="green lentils" />
          <item name="sun-dried tomatoes" />
          <item name="feta cheese" />
          <item name="arugula" />
          <item name="cucumber" />
          <item name="fresh dill" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
          <item name="lemon juice" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lentils are an excellent source of plant-based protein, fiber, iron, and folate; sun-dried tomatoes concentrate lycopene and add umami; feta provides calcium and tangy flavor; arugula offers vitamins A, C, and K. Olive oil and lemon create a heart-healthy dressing. High in fiber for digestive health and blood sugar management.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita</name>
        <short_description>Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Sauté fresh spinach with diced onion and garlic in olive oil until wilted. Mix with crumbled feta, a squeeze of lemon, dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stuff into warmed whole-wheat pita halves. Serve with a side of Greek salad or hummus and veggie sticks for a complete lunch.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-wheat pita" />
          <item name="spinach" />
          <item name="feta cheese" />
          <item name="onion" />
          <item name="garlic" />
          <item name="dill" />
          <item name="lemon" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Spinach is rich in iron, folate, and vitamins A and K; feta adds protein and calcium; whole-wheat pita provides fiber and complex carbohydrates. Olive oil contributes monounsaturated fats for heart health. A lighter take on traditional spanakopita without the phyllo pastry, reducing saturated fat while keeping all the classic flavors.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Lunch>
    <Dinner>
      <Meal>
        <name>Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables</name>
        <short_description>Flaky sea bass baked with a Provençal herb crust, served alongside roasted zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes drizzled with olive oil.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Season sea bass fillets with a mixture of breadcrumbs, dried herbs de Provence, lemon zest, and a drizzle of olive oil. Bake at 400°F for 15–18 minutes. Toss zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper; roast on a separate tray. Plate the fish over the vegetables and finish with a squeeze of lemon and fresh basil leaves.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="sea bass" />
          <item name="breadcrumbs" />
          <item name="herbs de Provence" />
          <item name="lemon" />
          <item name="zucchini" />
          <item name="eggplant" />
          <item name="cherry tomatoes" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
          <item name="fresh basil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Sea bass is a lean white fish high in protein and rich in selenium and B vitamins; roasted Mediterranean vegetables provide fiber, vitamins A and C, and potassium. Olive oil adds anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fats. Herbs de Provence (thyme, rosemary, oregano) contribute antioxidants. A light, elegant dinner aligned with the Mediterranean diet.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus</name>
        <short_description>Spiced lamb kofta patties served alongside a fresh bulgur tabbouleh salad and creamy homemade hummus — a hearty Mediterranean dinner spread.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Mix ground lamb with minced onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and fresh parsley; form into small oval patties and grill or pan-sear until cooked through. Prepare tabbouleh by tossing fine bulgur with chopped parsley, mint, tomato, and cucumber in a lemon-olive oil dressing. Serve with a generous scoop of hummus and warm flatbread.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="ground lamb" />
          <item name="bulgur wheat" />
          <item name="parsley" />
          <item name="mint" />
          <item name="tomato" />
          <item name="cucumber" />
          <item name="hummus" />
          <item name="cumin" />
          <item name="lemon" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lamb provides high-quality protein, iron, zinc, and B12; bulgur wheat is a whole grain rich in fiber and manganese; parsley and mint add vitamins A, C, and K plus fresh flavor. Hummus (chickpeas + tahini) supplies plant protein, fiber, and healthy fats. A satisfying, protein-rich dinner; opt for lean ground lamb to reduce saturated fat.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo</name>
        <short_description>Tender chicken thighs braised with orzo, tomatoes, olives, and artichoke hearts in a lemony broth — a comforting one-pot Mediterranean dinner.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Brown seasoned chicken thighs in olive oil in a deep skillet. Remove and sauté onion and garlic, then add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, orzo, sliced kalamata olives, and canned artichoke hearts. Nestle the chicken back in, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until orzo is tender and liquid is absorbed. Finish with lemon juice, crumbled feta, and chopped fresh oregano.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="chicken thighs" />
          <item name="orzo pasta" />
          <item name="diced tomatoes" />
          <item name="kalamata olives" />
          <item name="artichoke hearts" />
          <item name="chicken broth" />
          <item name="onion" />
          <item name="garlic" />
          <item name="feta cheese" />
          <item name="lemon" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Chicken thighs provide protein with more flavor than breast; orzo supplies carbohydrates; tomatoes and artichoke hearts are rich in fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; olives contribute monounsaturated fats. A balanced one-pot meal with protein, carbs, and vegetables. Use low-sodium broth to manage salt; skin-on thighs add richness but also saturated fat — remove skin if preferred.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Dinner>
  </Day>
  <Day index="2">
    <Breakfast>
      <Meal>
        <name>Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Mild-Spice Plate</name>
        <short_description>Creamy, mildly spiced apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats, milk, and apple.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Dairy-free option: use fortified plant milk. Boost protein by adding a tablespoon of nut butter or a sprinkle of chia/flax seeds. If extra sweetness is desired, drizzle honey or maple syrup. Top with extra sliced apple or a pinch more cinnamon for aroma.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="oats" />
          <item name="milk" />
          <item name="apple" />
          <item name="cinnamon" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Oats supply soluble fiber (beta-glucan) for fullness; milk provides protein and calcium; apple adds vitamins and natural sweetness; cinnamon enhances flavor with minimal added sugar. A balanced, warming breakfast; adjust dairy or sweetness as needed.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Ricotta Toast — Berry Boost Variation</name>
        <short_description>Ricotta toast with berries and a light honey drizzle—fiber-rich and protein-boosted for a quick, balanced meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Toast the whole-grain bread, spread ricotta, top with berries, and drizzle honey. Optional: sprinkle a pinch of cinnamon for extra flavor.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-grain bread" />
          <item name="ricotta" />
          <item name="honey" />
          <item name="berries" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein from ricotta; fiber from whole-grain bread and berries; natural sugars from honey and berries. A quick, balanced option—adjust honey to control sugar intake.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Miso Breakfast Set — Classic Set</name>
        <short_description>Protein-forward Japanese-inspired breakfast featuring miso, tofu, egg, green onion, and rice.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Prepare a warm miso broth and add tofu cubes and green onion; gently add or scramble an egg and cook to preferred doneness, then serve over steamed rice. Garnish with extra green onion for freshness.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="miso paste" />
          <item name="tofu" />
          <item name="green onion" />
          <item name="cooked rice" />
          <item name="egg" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich breakfast with tofu and egg, balanced by rice; provides energy from carbohydrates and healthy fats. Contains soy and is potentially high in sodium due to miso. For lower sodium, choose low-sodium miso and moderate portions.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Breakfast>
    <Lunch>
      <Meal>
        <name>Bean Burrito Bowl — Smoky Twist</name>
        <short_description>Bean Burrito Bowl — Smoky Twist with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Keep as-is for a balanced vegetarian bowl. For lower fat, use reduced-fat cheddar or reduce cheese; to increase fullness without many calories, add extra salsa or lettuce where possible.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="black beans" />
          <item name="rice" />
          <item name="corn" />
          <item name="lettuce" />
          <item name="salsa" />
          <item name="cheddar" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>High in fiber and plant protein from black beans; includes calcium from cheddar and energy from rice. Corn and lettuce add micronutrients; watch sodium and saturated fat from cheese and salsa; portion control helps with calories.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Bowl</name>
        <short_description>Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and a balsamic drizzle. Combines complex carbs, calcium-rich dairy, and fresh vegetables for a balanced meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve as a lunch with a side salad or fruit for extra vegetables and fiber. For more protein, add grilled chicken or chickpeas, or use a higher-protein cheese option.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-grain bread" />
          <item name="mozzarella" />
          <item name="tomato" />
          <item name="basil" />
          <item name="balsamic" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Calcium and protein from mozzarella; fiber from whole-grain bread; vitamins from tomato and basil; balsamic adds flavor with minimal calories. Moderate sodium from cheese; balanced mix of complex carbs, protein, and produce.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Chicken Noodle Soup — Garden Veg Bowl</name>
        <short_description>A warm garden-vegetable-inspired chicken noodle soup with tender chicken, noodles, carrots, and celery in savory broth.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve hot as a light main dish. To increase fiber and fullness, swap regular noodles for whole-grain noodles and add a handful of leafy greens or chopped spinach. Pair with a piece of fruit for a balanced meal.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="chicken" />
          <item name="noodles" />
          <item name="carrots" />
          <item name="celery" />
          <item name="broth" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Balanced mix of protein (chicken), carbohydrates (noodles), and vegetables (carrots, celery). Provides vitamins A and C from carrots and hydration from broth. For lower sodium, choose low-sodium broth and limit added salt; for extra fiber, use whole-grain noodles or add greens.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Lunch>
    <Dinner>
      <Meal>
        <name>Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Smoky Bake</name>
        <short_description>A one-pan sheet-pan dinner featuring smoky-tossed pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans roasted with olive oil for a balanced, convenient meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serving idea: 4 oz pork tenderloin, 1 cup potatoes, and 1 cup green beans, roasted with olive oil and smoky spices. Lightly season for flavor and pair with a simple leafy-green salad for extra volume and nutrients.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="pork tenderloin" />
          <item name="potatoes" />
          <item name="green beans" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean protein from pork tenderloin; fiber from potatoes and green beans; healthy fats from olive oil. Provides a balanced mix of protein, complex carbohydrates, and micronutrients; can reduce calories by using a smaller amount of oil and trimming visible fat.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Roast Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Variation</name>
        <short_description>Protein-forward roast dinner featuring chicken, quinoa, and vegetables, with an optional berry-backed antioxidant boost.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, steamed broccoli, and carrots. For Berry Boost, add a side of mixed berries or a small berry glaze to introduce antioxidants and a touch of sweetness without overpowering the savory components.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="chicken thighs" />
          <item name="quinoa" />
          <item name="broccoli" />
          <item name="carrots" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich roast chicken thighs paired with fiber from quinoa and vegetables. Broccoli and carrots provide vitamins C and A, plus minerals. Berry Boost variation can include a berry side or light glaze to add antioxidants; use unsweetened berries to minimize added sugars.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Berry Boost Bake</name>
        <short_description>A quick sheet-pan dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans roasted with olive oil for a balanced meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan using olive oil; season lightly and pair with a cup of fresh berries or a berry yogurt for additional flavor and antioxidants.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="pork tenderloin" />
          <item name="potatoes" />
          <item name="green beans" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes contribute energy-rich carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil supplies heart-healthy fats. For a balanced serving, aim for about 3–4 oz pork, 1 cup potatoes, and 1 cup green beans, with olive oil portion kept moderate. Add berries on the side to boost vitamins and antioxidants if desired.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Dinner>
  </Day>
  <Day index="3">
    <Breakfast>
      <Meal>
        <name>Fruit & Nut Plate — Smoky Combo</name>
        <short_description>A balanced snack of mixed fruit, yogurt, and nuts delivering fiber, protein, and healthy fats.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Assemble a serving with 1 cup mixed fruit, ¼–½ cup plain Greek yogurt, and a small handful (about ¼ cup) nuts; enjoy as a portable, balanced snack and adjust portions to fit your daily needs.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="mixed fruit" />
          <item name="nuts" />
          <item name="yogurt" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Provides fiber and antioxidants from fruit, protein and calcium from yogurt, and healthy fats from nuts. To keep sugar and calories in check, choose plain yogurt and unsalted nuts.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Overnight Oats — Citrus Fresh Twist</name>
        <short_description>Creamy overnight oats with milk, plain yogurt, apple and cinnamon; a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast that can be brightened with a citrus note.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>For a true Citrus Fresh Twist, add citrus zest (orange or lemon) or a small splash of juice to brighten the oats. Top with chopped nuts or seeds for healthy fats and extra crunch. Consider vanilla yogurt or a touch of vanilla extract for depth.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="rolled oats" />
          <item name="milk" />
          <item name="plain yogurt" />
          <item name="apple" />
          <item name="cinnamon" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Balanced breakfast with protein and calcium from yogurt and milk, plus fiber from oats and apple. No added sugar in the base ingredients; cinnamon adds flavor and may support blood sugar regulation. Calories vary with dairy choices but it's a filling, nutrient-dense start to the day.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Whole-Grain Pancakes — Citrus Fresh Variation</name>
        <short_description>Whole-grain pancakes with egg, milk, berries, and maple syrup—fiber-rich, protein-containing breakfast with natural sweetness.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve with a small yogurt or extra berries for added protein or fiber. To echo the citrus theme, garnish with orange zest or add a splash of citrus juice to the batter.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-wheat flour" />
          <item name="egg" />
          <item name="milk" />
          <item name="berries" />
          <item name="maple syrup" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Fiber-rich whole-wheat batter with egg and milk provides sustained energy and high-quality protein; berries add vitamins, fiber, and antioxidants; maple syrup adds sweetness with natural sugars. The Citrus Fresh Variation name suggests optional citrus zest or juice for extra vitamin C and bright flavor, not included in the base ingredients.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Superfood Breakfast — Superfood Boost Bowl</name>
        <short_description>A nutrient-dense superfood bowl with spinach-kale smoothie base, chia-hemp crunch, mixed berries, sliced banana, toasted almonds, and a drizzle of almond butter — high in antioxidants, fiber, and omega-3s.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Blend a base of baby spinach and kale with 1/2 frozen banana, 1/2 cup mixed berries, 1/2 cup unsweetened almond milk, and 1 tablespoon ground flaxseed until smooth. Pour into a bowl and top with 2 tablespoons chia seeds mixed with a tablespoon of hemp seeds for crunch, sliced banana, a small handful of toasted almonds, and a drizzle (1 tsp) of almond butter. Optionally sprinkle with a few goji berries and a pinch of cinnamon. Serve immediately for a refreshing, micronutrient-rich start to the day.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="baby spinach" />
          <item name="kale" />
          <item name="banana" />
          <item name="mixed berries" />
          <item name="unsweetened almond milk" />
          <item name="ground flaxseed" />
          <item name="chia seeds" />
          <item name="hemp seeds" />
          <item name="toasted almonds" />
          <item name="almond butter" />
          <item name="goji berries" />
          <item name="cinnamon" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>This superfood bowl delivers antioxidants (berries, goji), fiber (chia, flax, fruits, greens), plant-based omega-3s (ground flaxseed, chia), and a modest amount of protein and healthy fats (hemp seeds, almonds, almond butter). The greens provide vitamins K, A, and folate; berries offer vitamin C and polyphenols. It's a low-sodium, nutrient-packed breakfast ideal for energy, recovery, and long-term health. Adjust portion sizes for additional calories or add a scoop of protein powder if more protein is desired.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Breakfast>
    <Lunch>
      <Meal>
        <name>Egg Fried Rice — Maple-Kissed Skillet</name>
        <short_description>Savory egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve with a side of steamed greens or add tofu/chicken for extra protein. Try brown rice for more fiber. Use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate sesame oil to reduce sodium and fat.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="rice" />
          <item name="egg" />
          <item name="peas" />
          <item name="carrots" />
          <item name="soy sauce" />
          <item name="sesame oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>A balanced quick fried rice dish: carbohydrates from the rice, protein from the egg, and fiber from peas and carrots. Contains healthy fats from sesame oil. Sodium can be high due to soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or reducing the amount. For more fiber, swap to brown rice; for more protein, add another egg or lean protein on the side.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Hummus Snack Plate — Citrus Fresh Style</name>
        <short_description>Colorful Mediterranean-inspired snack plate featuring hummus, whole-wheat pita, veggies, and a cheese component for protein, fiber, and healthy fats.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>As a Citrus Fresh-style snack: assemble with 2–3 tablespoons hummus, 1 small whole-wheat pita cut into wedges, cucumber slices, cherry tomatoes, and a small cheese wedge. Add a squeeze of lemon or citrus zest to brighten the flavors. If dairy-free, omit cheese or substitute with a plant-based option.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="hummus" />
          <item name="whole-wheat pita" />
          <item name="cucumber" />
          <item name="cherry tomatoes" />
          <item name="cheese" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>This plate provides protein from hummus and cheese, plus fiber from whole-wheat pita and vegetables, and healthy fats from tahini in hummus. It offers a balanced mix of complex carbs, protein, and fats. Watch sodium from store-bought hummus and cheese; consider reduced-sodium options or smaller portions if needed. For a lighter version, you can reduce cheese or use part-skim options.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Veggie Quesadilla — Classic Bake</name>
        <short_description>A baked veggie quesadilla with whole-wheat tortilla, cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve with a side salad or fruit. For a lighter version, use part-skim cheese or reduce cheese amount; add avocado for healthy fats and extra fiber.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-wheat tortilla" />
          <item name="cheddar" />
          <item name="bell pepper" />
          <item name="spinach" />
          <item name="salsa" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; protein and calcium from cheddar. Spinach and bell pepper supply vitamins A and C; salsa adds flavor with relatively few calories. If watching sodium, choose low-sodium cheese or salsa.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Lunch>
    <Dinner>
      <Meal>
        <name>Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Berry Boost Combo</name>
        <short_description>Turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in marinara, topped with parmesan; Berry Boost indicates a berry-based fruit side for added antioxidants.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve with a side of mixed berries or a small berry compote to fit the Berry Boost concept; add steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="whole-wheat pasta" />
          <item name="marinara" />
          <item name="parmesan" />
        </ingredients>

The Berry Boost name suggests pairing with a berry fruit side for extra antioxidants and vitamins.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Roast Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Twist</name>
        <short_description>Roasted chicken dinner with quinoa and vegetables (broccoli and carrots), adaptable with a berry-enhanced twist for extra antioxidants.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Suggested portion: 4–6 oz cooked chicken thighs; 1/2–3/4 cup cooked quinoa; 1 cup broccoli; 1 cup carrots. Optional: include a side of berries or a berry glaze to fit the Berry Boost Twist.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="chicken thighs" />
          <item name="quinoa" />
          <item name="broccoli" />
          <item name="carrots" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich with chicken thighs and quinoa; fiber and vitamins from broccoli and carrots. Berry Boost Twist can be realized by adding fresh berries or a berry sauce on the side for extra antioxidants.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Garden Veg Style</name>
        <short_description>Sheet-pan pork dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, green beans, and olive oil.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan, drizzled with olive oil and seasoned with garlic and herbs for a simple, nutritious dinner.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="pork tenderloin" />
          <item name="potatoes" />
          <item name="green beans" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean pork provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a balanced portion, aim for about 4–6 oz pork with 1–2 cups vegetables and a moderate potato serving per person.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Dinner>

  <Day index="4">
    <Breakfast>
      <Meal>
        <name>Miso Breakfast Set — Sesame Variation</name>
        <short_description>Miso breakfast set with tofu, egg, green onion, rice, and optional sesame variation for a protein-packed savory morning meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Serve warm as a balanced bowl: mix in the tofu with a small amount of miso for flavor, top with green onion and a gently cooked egg, and accompany with steamed rice. If sesame variation is used, finish with toasted sesame seeds for extra texture; opt for low-sodium miso to keep sodium in check.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="miso paste" />
          <item name="tofu" />
          <item name="green onion" />
          <item name="cooked rice" />
          <item name="egg" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich breakfast from tofu and egg supports satiety and muscle maintenance. Includes rice for steady energy; miso provides fermentation-associated flavor and potential gut benefits. Sodium intake can be high from miso paste—consider low-sodium miso if needed. Sesame variation may add healthy fats and a nutty flavor if sesame is used.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Breakfast Sandwich — Sesame Bowl</name>
        <short_description>A savory breakfast sandwich on a whole-wheat English muffin with egg, cheddar, and tomato.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Quick, balanced breakfast. To adjust: add another egg or more cheese for extra protein; use egg whites and light cheese to lower calories; choose a higher-fiber muffin to boost fiber.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="egg" />
          <item name="cheddar" />
          <item name="tomato" />

      </Meal>
      <Meal>

        <short_description>Smoky breakfast wrap with egg, turkey, spinach in a whole-wheat tortilla and salsa.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>For lower sodium, use low-sodium turkey and salsa; add avocado for healthy fats to enhance fullness and flavor.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="egg" />
          <item name="turkey slices" />
          <item name="spinach" />
          <item name="salsa" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Balanced breakfast wrap with lean protein (turkey and egg) and fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach. Monitor sodium from turkey and salsa; consider low-sodium options if needed.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>

    <Lunch>
      <Meal>
        <name>Turkey Sandwich — Classic Set</name>
        <short_description>Classic turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Pair with a piece of fruit and a small yogurt for a complete lunch; add extra veggie slices or a side salad to boost fiber.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="turkey slices" />
          <item name="lettuce" />
          <item name="tomato" />
          <item name="mustard" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich turkey and fiber from whole-grain bread provide a balanced base with vitamins from lettuce and tomato. Low in fat; sodium can be moderate—opt for low-sodium turkey and mustard if salt is a concern.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic Variation</name>
        <short_description>Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic variation with chicken, rice, mixed vegetables, and teriyaki sauce.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Aim for a balanced portion: 3–4 oz cooked chicken, 1 cup cooked brown rice, and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables. Use low-sodium teriyaki or dilute the sauce with water, and finish with sesame seeds or sliced green onions for flavor without excess calories.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="chicken" />
          <item name="rice" />
          <item name="mixed vegetables" />
          <item name="teriyaki sauce" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein-rich chicken supports satiety; rice provides energy; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals. Teriyaki sauce contributes flavor but can add sodium and added sugars. For a healthier option, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa, and use a low-sodium or diluted sauce to reduce sodium intake.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Grilled Cheese & Soup — Lemon-Herb Variation</name>
        <short_description>Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, served with tomato soup and grapes—a simple, fiber-rich meal with protein, calcium, and fruit.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>For a lighter option, use low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheddar; keep the whole-grain bread for fiber. Pair with a side salad or steamed veggies to boost micronutrients and volume without excessive calories.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="whole-grain bread" />
          <item name="cheddar" />
          <item name="tomato soup" />
          <item name="grapes" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Fiber from whole-grain bread and grapes; calcium and protein from cheddar; tomato soup adds vegetables. Moderate sodium from cheese and soup; consider low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheese to lower sodium and saturated fat. The lemon-herb variation isn't reflected in the listed ingredients; to capture that flavor, add lemon zest and herbs to the sandwich or soup.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
    </Lunch>
    <Dinner>
      <Meal>
        <name>Vegetable Soup & Bread — Lemon-Herb Bowl</name>
        <short_description>Plant-based meal: vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad—light, fiber-rich, and satisfying.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Boost protein by adding beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or salad; use a low-sodium soup; drizzle the salad with olive oil and lemon for healthy fats.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="side salad" />

      </Meal>

          <item name="ground turkey" />

          <item name="tomato sauce" />
          <item name="cheddar" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; rice supplies quick energy carbs; bell peppers add fiber and vitamin C. Cheddar adds calcium and flavor, contributing some saturated fat and sodium depending on cheese and sauce. For a lighter version, choose low-sodium tomato sauce and lean turkey.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Combo</name>
        <short_description>Tofu and mixed vegetables in curry sauce over rice, a quick, plant-based curry meal with citrusy freshness.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or serve with a side salad; add a squeeze of lemon or lime for extra citrus brightness.</meal_suggestion>

          <item name="mixed vegetables" />
          <item name="curry sauce" />

    </Dinner>

      <Meal>
        <name>Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet</name>
        <short_description>One-pan sheet-pan dinner: pork tenderloin roasted with potatoes and green beans in olive oil for a simple, balanced meal.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss potatoes and green beans with olive oil, salt, and pepper, and spread on a sheet pan. Rub pork tenderloin with herbs and a light coat of oil, place on the pan. Roast 25–30 minutes until the pork reaches 145°F (63°C) and the potatoes are tender. Rest pork 3–5 minutes, then slice and serve with the vegetables.</meal_suggestion>
        <ingredients>
          <item name="pork tenderloin" />
          <item name="potatoes" />
          <item name="green beans" />
          <item name="olive oil" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes offer complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a lighter version, use minimal added salt and portion appropriately. Let the pork rest after cooking to retain juiciness.</nutrition_note>

          <item name="quinoa" />
          <item name="broccoli" />
          <item name="carrots" />
        </ingredients>
        <nutrition_note>Protein from chicken thighs; quinoa adds complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots provide vitamins, minerals, and additional fiber. For a leaner option, use skinless thighs.</nutrition_note>
      </Meal>
      <Meal>
        <name>Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake</name>
        <short_description>Baked chicken with mixed vegetables and rice glazed in teriyaki sauce for a balanced, savory dinner.</short_description>
        <meal_suggestion>Meal prep idea: bake a larger batch and portion into 4 servings. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa.           <item name="mixed vegetables" />
          <item name="rice" />
          <item name="teriyaki sauce" />

For more fiber, substitute brown rice and/or increase vegetable portions.</nutrition_note>
